note_text: My dog Boss is about 8 years old.

expect.rationale: Age is relative-to-now and goes stale, so v20 resolves it to an approximate birthDate attribute, never a static `age` value (which also misfires the new_predicate card on the unknown `age` spelling). The exact birth year depends on the capture anchor, so only the predicate/kind and the ABSENCE of `age` are asserted; advisory because the precision (year vs era) is model latitude.

note_text: I worked at the US Army from 2008 to 2016, and I currently work at SpaceX.

expect.rationale: An explicit dated range ('from 2008 to 2016') is a CLOSED interval (both bounds set), so the past job records as bounded history (former) beside the current open job — the two coexist as a work history rather than the past job staying open and forcing a functional-worksFor fact_conflict. Advisory because real-Grok temporal resolution of the range end is the thing under test.
